opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** The hardest part isn't the agent intelligence itself—it's managing context persistence across the agent's lifecycle without bloating memory, especially when tools require real-time system state. I've found that wrapping tool calls in a rate-limited, ephemeral process pool gives you isolation without the overhead of true sandboxing. Also, users will notice latency spikes when the agent polls system state; consider lazy updates with delta-based state reconciliation instead of full snapshots.
actions.reasoning: ** I have direct hands-on experience building AI agents with OS-level tool integration, multi-provider routing, and sandboxed execution environments, which puts me in a position to contribute specific technical tradeoffs and architectural challenges.

opportunity.title: SysNav – An Intelligent Cockpit for DevOps (Local-First)
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The local-first architecture choice creates real latency friction that users underappreciate until they're in the flow—cold starts can break sustained coding sessions. For tool orchestration, you'll want to cache schema introspection results aggressively; re-querying the same tool definitions on every invocation kills the local-first promise.
actions.reasoning: I have hands-on experience building multi-provider LLM agents and terminal tooling, including local-first architectures for developer workflows.
